pr.title: lb: implement lb_80019628; improve four lbbgflash functions
pr.author: itsgrimetime

summary: Implemented a first C body for `lb_80019628`, moving it from asm-only to 82.58% fuzzy, and made MWCC-oriented matching tweaks in `lbbgflash.c`. The main reusable value is compiler-control tactics: scoped exact-type locals, source-order arithmetic changes, and a narrow `volatile` pointer to preserve inline assert code; no function reached 100% match in this PR.

key_files.role: Added the C implementation of `lb_80019628`, a pad-sampling-rate and periodic-alarm maintenance routine called from `lb_80019894`.
Adjusted `fn_8001FC08`, `lbBgFlash_80020E38`, and `fn_8002113C` for closer MWCC output; PR body also claims a `fn_80020AEC` improvement, but the available diff slice does not show that hunk.

what_changed.details: Added a C body that snapshots `lb_804329F0.x38`, updates `x0[0].x0`, resets `x0[0].x8` if it is out of range, chooses a period from `x0[0].x0`, `x0[1].x0`, `OS_TIMER_CLOCK`, and a 1/60-second cap, then updates `PADSetSamplingRate` and reschedules `lb_804329F0.alarm` through `OSSetPeriodicAlarm(..., (OSAlarmHandler) fn_800195FC)`.
what_changed.why_it_matters: Replaced the asm-only fallback with an 82.58% fuzzy source implementation for pad sampling/alarm timing logic.

what_changed.details: Rewrote each RGBA saturation block to introduce branch-local `u8 target` and `f32* cur` temporaries before comparing and storing channel values.
what_changed.why_it_matters: PR body says this keeps the byte target and `x10[i]` pointer in the desired registers across rising/falling logic, improving fuzzy match from about 89.6% to 91.77%.

what_changed.details: Changed squared-component local order from `dz2`, `dx2`, `dy2` to `dx2`, `dy2`, `dz2`, and changed the zero-vector sum from `dz2 + dy2 + dx2` to `dx2 + dy2 + dz2`.
what_changed.why_it_matters: Algebraically equivalent source order better matched the original PPC floating-point pipeline scheduling; bot reported 98.46% to 98.52% fuzzy.

what_changed.details: Introduced `Quaternion* volatile rot_ptr = &rot;` and routed `Fake_HSD_JObjGetRotation`, `HSD_MkRotationMtx`, `HSD_QuatLib_8037EB28`, and `FakeHSD_JObjSetRotation` through it in the non-`JOBJ_USE_QUATERNION` path.
what_changed.why_it_matters: The volatile pointer prevents MWCC from proving `&rot` non-null and eliding inline-substituted asserts in `Fake_HSD_JObjGetRotation`; bot reported 93.17% to 94.72% fuzzy.

what_changed.details: PR body claims a ternary parent assignment was expanded to explicit `if/else` to match a `bne / b / li` branch schedule.
what_changed.why_it_matters: Evidence is weak in the provided slice: the diff file does not show a `fn_80020AEC` hunk and the bot report lists a `.sdata` improvement instead.

smart_moves: Used early returns in `lb_80019628` when `lb_804329F0.x38` or the computed period is unchanged, avoiding unnecessary alarm and sampling-rate churn while matching control flow.

Kept timer math type-sensitive: casts through `(f64) (u32) OS_TIMER_CLOCK`, a `0.016666668f * (f32) (u32) OS_TIMER_CLOCK` 60 FPS cap, and `(u64) period / (OS_TIMER_CLOCK / 1000)` for the rate calculation.
Capped the computed pad sampling rate at 11 before calling `PADSetSamplingRate`, preserving the PR body’s described millisecond cap behavior.
Introduced tiny branch-local `u8 target` and `f32* cur` locals in `fn_8001FC08` to influence MWCC register allocation without changing RGBA saturation semantics.
Matched commutative floating-point code by reordering local declarations and sum operands, acknowledging that MWCC/PPC instruction scheduling depends on source order.
Used a narrowly scoped `Quaternion* volatile` workaround to keep inline assert calls alive instead of globally weakening optimization.
decomp_lessons: For MWCC matching, semantically equivalent direct field accesses may not allocate registers like the original; scoped pointer and exact-width locals can be enough to move codegen.
Commutative arithmetic is not free in decomp work: reorder declarations and operands to match observed `lfs`, `fmuls`, and `fadds` scheduling.
`volatile` can be a targeted matching tool when the compiler is optimizing away assert paths because it can prove a stack address is non-null.
When implementing timer/state-machine routines, preserve signed/unsigned comparison shapes (`u64`, `s64`, `OSTime`) and cast order; these affect both correctness and emitted code.
Keep verification tied to the whole DOL and sibling-function stability; this PR reports `python configure.py && ninja` green with `build/GALE01/main.dol: OK` and unchanged sibling functions.
Bot fuzzy percentages are useful progress evidence, but PR body and bot output can disagree; record discrepancies instead of assuming all claimed functions are confirmed by the diff.
naming_conventions: Address-based library symbols remain in use: `lb_80019628`, `fn_800195FC`, `lb_80019880`, `lb_80019894`, `fn_8001FC08`, `lbBgFlash_80020E38`, `fn_8002113C`, `fn_80020AEC`.
Struct fields are still offset-style for unknown lb state: `lb_804329F0.x38`, `x40`, `x48`, `x4`, `x0[0].x0`, `x0[0].x8`, `x0[1].x0`.
Color/flash data uses mixed known and offset-style names: `data->x8.r`, `data->x8.g`, `data->x8.b`, `data->x8.a`, `data->x10[i]`, `data->x20[i]`.
HSD rotation helpers retain explicit fake-wrapper names where assert behavior matters: `Fake_HSD_JObjGetRotation` and `FakeHSD_JObjSetRotation`.

Nintendo/HSD API names are preserved verbatim: `OSAlarm`, `OSTime`, `OS_TIMER_CLOCK`, `PADSetSamplingRate`, `OSCancelAlarm`, `OSCreateAlarm`, `OSSetPeriodicAlarm`, `OSAlarmHandler`, `HSD_JObj`, `JOBJ_USE_QUATERNION`, `Quaternion`, `Vec3`.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Use branch-local `u8 target` plus `f32* cur` in repeated channel clamp code to keep the byte target and current-value pointer live in the desired registers (`r5`/`r4` per PR body).
Use source-order local declarations like `dx2`, `dy2`, `dz2` and sum operands in the original order to tune PPC floating-point scheduling.
Route stack addresses through `Quaternion* volatile` to block MWCC non-null proof and preserve inline assert code at `jobj.h:699/700`.
For HSD/JObj rotation paths, maintain the same helper sequence: get rotation, build matrix, concatenate, convert back, set rotation.
For OS alarm routines, keep explicit alarm pointer locals and explicit `(OSAlarmHandler)` casts when rescheduling periodic alarms.
PR body suggests replacing a ternary with explicit `if/else` can change branch order to match `bne / b / li`, but this specific `fn_80020AEC` claim is not visible in the provided diff.

review_feedback.feedback: Reported five improvements in unmatched items for GALE01: `lb_80019628` +495 bytes 0.00% to 82.58%, `fn_8001FC08` +15 bytes 89.68% to 91.77%, `fn_8002113C` +11 bytes 93.17% to 94.72%, `lbbgflash` `.sdata` +6 bytes 50.00% to 65.71%, and `lbBgFlash_80020E38` 98.46% to 98.52%.

Author reported no 100% matches, but five improvements toward original output; verification was `python configure.py && ninja` green with `build/GALE01/main.dol: OK` and all sibling functions in both translation units unchanged.
No human review comments or review records were present in the slice; PR was merged shortly after creation.
The PR body mentions a `fn_80020AEC` fuzzy improvement, but the available diff excerpt/full slice does not show a `fn_80020AEC` code change and the bot comment lists `.sdata` instead.

follow_up_queries: Search for callers of `lb_80019628` and `lb_80019894` to validate the `lb_804329F0.x38/x40/x48` pad-sampling state machine.
Compare the original asm for `lb_80019628` around `OS_TIMER_CLOCK`, the 1/60 cap, and `PADSetSamplingRate` to see what remains blocking a full match.
Try the `u8 target` plus `f32* cur` scoped-local pattern in other RGBA or channel-saturation routines with repeated offset-array stores.
Look for other `Fake_HSD_JObjGetRotation` or inline HSD assert sites where a narrow `volatile` pointer may preserve original assert calls.
Verify the `fn_80020AEC` claim against commit history or upstream diff, since the PR body mentions an if/else parent scheduling tweak not shown in this slice.
